channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Why AI disinformation hasn’t moved the needle in the 2024 election
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: This agency is tasked with protecting elections from cyber attacks. If Trump wins, it could be in danger
item.description: Jen Easterly’s confirmation by the Senate in July 2021 to become director of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) was about as smooth as Senate votes get. With a résumé that includes time in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Max Ufberg, Fast Company

item.title: Google's partnership with AI startup Anthropic faces a UK competition investigation
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s competition watchdog said Thursday it's opening a formal investigation into Google’s partnership with artificial intelligence startup Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Seven cyber resilience recommendations for DoD mission continuity and data recovery
item.description: The DoD must prioritize data recovery and backup strategies to counter escalating cyber threats and build cyber resilience. The post Seven cyber resilience recommendations for DoD mission continuity and data recovery first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Michael Keaton: Trump, Musk don't respect supporters
item.description: Actor Michael Keaton said former President Trump and tech billionaire Elon Musk don’t respect their supporters, in a video posted to Instagram this week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Supermicro unveils AI-optimized storage powered by Nvidia
item.description: Supermicro announced the launch of a new storage system optimized for AI workloads using multiple Nvidia BlueField-3 data processing units (DPU) combined with an all-flash array. The new Just a Bunch of Flash (JBOF) system features a 2U rack that can house up to four BlueField-3 DPUs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nigeria releases American crypto executive after dropping money laundering case
item.description: An American cryptocurrency executive held in Nigeria for the past eight months has been released after authorities there announced that they were ending his money laundering trial on health and diplomatic grounds.
item.dc:creator: Chinedu Asadu And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AI is now a crucial tool in Trump’s image-management arsenal
item.description: An image of Donald Trump cosplaying as a football player last weekend set the tone for the final push of the Republican nominee’s campaign. It depicts Trump as a yoked, veiny-arm baller glowering from within a Steelers uniform on the field at Pittsburgh’s Acrisure Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: PSA: Here are Netflix’s secret codes to see all its horror movies and shows
item.description: Tired of endlessly scrolling on Netflix? These secret codes will help you find horror movies and shows in minutes.
item.dc:creator: lucy.buglass@futurenet.com (Lucy Buglass), TechRadar

item.title: Roblox to enact child-safety changes giving parents more control
item.description: The changes follow Roblox’s July announcement that it would label games based on the type of content they contain, rather than by age.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple M4 MacBook Air Models in Production for 2025 Launch, M4 Mac Studio Pushed Back
item.description: Apple's highly anticipated M4 MacBook Air models are rumored to be in production for launch in 2025, with the brand's M4 Mac Studio reportedly now being pushed back and becoming a secondary focus.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Musk voter lottery might violate federal law: DOJ
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has warned Elon Musk’s pro-Trump super PAC that its daily $1 million giveaways to registered voters in swing states might violate federal law, according to several news outlets.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Not your father’s avatar: The real future of artificial intelligence
item.description: Generative artificial intelligence (genAI) is the latest milestone in the “AAA” journey, which began with the automation of the mundane, lead to augmentation — mostly machine-driven but lately also expanding into human augmentation — and has built up to artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Russia amplified hurricane disinformation to drive Americans apart, researchers find
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Russia has helped amplify and spread false and misleading internet claims about recent hurricanes in the United States and the federal government's response, part of a wider effort by the Kremlin to manipulate America's political discourse before the presidential election, new rese
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New rules for US national security agencies balance AI's promise with need to protect against risks
item.description: New rules from the White House on the use of artificial intelligence by U.S. national security and spy agencies aim to balance the technology’s promise with the need to protect against its risks.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 2025: The year of the AI PC
item.description: As PC, chip, and other component makers unveil products tailored to generative artificial intelligence (genAI) needs on edge devices, users can expect to see far more task automation and copilot-like assistants embedded on desktops and laptops next year.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Companies to shift AI goals in 2025 — with setbacks inevitable, Forrester predicts
item.description: Forrester Research this week unleashed a slate of predictions for 2025. Noting that companies pursued bold experiments in 2024 driven by generative AI and other emerging technologies, the research and advisory firm predicts a pivot to realizing value.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Intel scores fresh win against EU after top court backs annulment of billion-euro antitrust fine
item.description: Chipmaker Intel has won a fresh victory in a long-running battle against European Union competition watchdogs after the bloc’s top court confirmed a lower tribunal’s decision to overturn a billion-euro antitrust penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Enter the Neoverse with Azure’s Cobalt servers
item.description: Microsoft has been running Arm servers as part of Azure for some time now, initially with Altera Ampere hardware, but now with its own Cobalt 100 hardware. The Cobalt processors are based on Arm’s Neoverse architecture and were first announced nearly a year ago, with a limited preview.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A medida que se escala la IA surgen retos de infraestructura
item.description: Las organizaciones que experimentan con la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa suelen crear cuentas de nivel empresarial con servicios basados en la nube como ChatGPT de OpenAI o Claude de Anthropic, y las primeras pruebas de campo y los beneficios en productividad pueden inspirarles a buscar má
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cash App founder's ex-wife slams alleged killer sister's testimony
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Bob Lee's ex-wife did not hold back her feelings toward the accused killer's sister at the courthouse on Wednesday. "My husband was murdered. She has no right to make herself a victim in this.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: Meta Oversight Board seriously concerned about Facebook’s ‘overenforcement’ of Harris-Walz nipple post
item.description: Meta Platforms’ Oversight Board on Wednesday told the company to keep up a Facebook post superimposing the faces of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Two new HHS programs to help with complicated health challenges
item.description: "A lot of the efforts that we're doing across the government have a plug into the artificial intelligence world," said Andy Kilianski. The post Two new HHS programs to help with complicated health challenges first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Gartner: 13 AI insights for enterprise IT
item.description: Artificial intelligence is an early stage technology and the hype around it is palpable, but IT leaders need to take many challenges into consideration before making major commitments for their enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Qualcomm’s license battle with Arm puts many AI-enabled Copilot+ PCs in peril
item.description: As its long-running dispute with Arm turned into a war of words this week, the stakes for chip giant Qualcomm and its technology partners, including Microsoft, couldn’t be higher. Along with MediaTek and Apple, Qualcomm is one of the biggest suppliers of chips for use in smartphones and tablets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: This viral Tim Walz content was created by Russia, U.S. intelligence confirms
item.description: Groups in Russia created and helped spread viral disinformation targeting Democratic vice presidential candidate Tim Walz, a senior U.S. intelligence official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: How developers can automate ‘computer use’ with Anthropic’s new LLM
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude 3.5 Sonnet large language model has gained a new ability: operating a computer. The new ability, which the company is calling “computer use,” is currently in beta test.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to prepare your online accounts for when you die
item.description: Most people have accumulated a pile of data on their social media and digital accounts over their lifetimes.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Excitement growing over GSA’s COMET II program
item.description: GSA is putting the final touches on its next generation IT services contract, called COMET II, that is expected to draw dozens of bidders. The post Excitement growing over GSA’s COMET II program first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Hydrogen startup Verdagy opens San Jose plant at risky moment
item.description: The Silicon Valley company is firing up its electrolyzer factory even as hydrogen demand has been slow to grow.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: PETRILA, Romania (AP) — For many years, Sebastian Tirinticǎ worked in a coal mine, just like his father and grandfather before him.
item.dc:creator: ANCA GURZU, Cipher News, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Artificial intelligence's recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should use the technology and whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: AI needs Apple, which is why time is on its side
item.description: Apple was behind on smartphones, until it wasn’t. It lagged the crowd in digital music players, until it didn’t. There was a time when it was woeful on wearables, until it that changed.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IBM works to address the developer skills gap with AI
item.description: When IBM announced in May 2023 that its generative artificial intelligence tools were in use by its AI assistant, watsonx, to help enterprises modernize their mainframe applications by translating COBOL code into Java, it was just the beginning of its efforts to leverage generative AI for developers
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Rising crypto scams leave Americans reeling from billions in losses
item.description: Scammers have illustrated that cryptocurrency is not as foolproof as it has promised. Americans lost $5.6 billion to crypto scams in 2023, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, NewsNation
